Concept Schemec Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les classes

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#ConceptScheme

A set of concepts, optionally including statements about semantic relationships between those concepts.
Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core
Exemple 
Thesauri, classification schemes, subject heading lists, taxonomies, 'folksonomies', and other types of controlled vocabulary are all examples of concept schemes. Concept schemes are also embedded in glossaries and terminologies.
a pour super-classes
Thing c
a pour domaine
has top concept op
a pour cible
is in scheme op, is top concept in scheme op
est disjoint avec
Collection c, Concept c

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2003/01/geo/wgs84_pos#Point

Uniquely identified by lat/long/alt. i.e. spaciallyIntersects(P1, P2) :- lat(P1, LAT), long(P1, LONG), alt(P1, ALT), lat(P2, LAT), long(P2, LONG), alt(P2, ALT). sameThing(P1, P2) :- type(P1, Point), type(P2, Point), spaciallyIntersects(P1, P2).

Research Infrastructurec Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les classes

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#ResearchInfrastructure

A research infrastructure may bring together one or more Regional Facilities
The following definition for RI from Article 2 (6) of the Regulation (EU) No 1291/2013 of 11 December 2013: `Establishing Horizon 2020 - the Framework Programme for Research and Innovation (2014- 2020)` applies: “RI are facilities, resources and services that are used by the research communities to conduct research and foster innovation in their fields. They include: major scientific equipment (or sets of instruments), knowledge-based resources such as collections, archives and scientific data, einfrastructures, such as data and computing systems and communication networks and any other tools that are essential to achieve excellence in research and innovation.” Accordingly, RI are implemented along different organisational models, including central sources and laboratories for experiments and measurement sessions, coordination and management of geographically distributed observatories or laboratories, remotely accessible resources for computing, data banks, physical sample repositories, surveys and longitudinal studies.
a pour super-classes
Organisation c
a pour domaine
Research Infrastructure is led by op, a pour pays op, homepage op, is managed by op
a pour cible
ERIC federated a Research Infrastructure op, Regional Facility is associated with a Research Infrastructure op, Regional Facility is part of a Research Infrastructure op, Regional Facility is related to a Research Infrastructure op, Site is part of an Research Infrastructure op, Subsite is part of an Research Infrastructure op
a pour membres
E M S O Italia ni, EMBRC-France ni, EMSO Portugal ni, EMSO-France ni, NorEMSO ni

has broaderop Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les propriétés d'objet

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#broader

Broader concepts are typically rendered as parents in a concept hierarchy (tree).
Relates a concept to a concept that is more general in meaning.
Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core
a pour super-propriétés
has broader transitive op
a pour sous-propriétés
has broader match op
est inverse de
has narrower op

has broader matchop Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les propriétés d'objet

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#broadMatch

skos:broadMatch is used to state a hierarchical mapping link between two conceptual resources in different concept schemes.
Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core
a pour super-propriétés
has broader op, is in mapping relation with op
est inverse de
has narrower match op

has broader transitiveop Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les propriétés d'objet

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#broaderTransitive

skos:broaderTransitive is a transitive superproperty of skos:broader.
Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core

a pour caractéristiques: transitive

a pour super-propriétés
is in semantic relation with op
a pour sous-propriétés
has broader op
est inverse de
has narrower transitive op

has close matchop Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les propriétés d'objet

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#closeMatch

skos:closeMatch is used to link two concepts that are sufficiently similar that they can be used interchangeably in some information retrieval applications. In order to avoid the possibility of "compound errors" when combining mappings across more than two concept schemes, skos:closeMatch is not declared to be a transitive property.
Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core

a pour caractéristiques: symétrique

a pour super-propriétés
is in mapping relation with op
a pour sous-propriétés
has exact match op

has exact matchop Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les propriétés d'objet

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#exactMatch

skos:exactMatch is disjoint with each of the properties skos:broadMatch and skos:relatedMatch.
skos:exactMatch is used to link two concepts, indicating a high degree of confidence that the concepts can be used interchangeably across a wide range of information retrieval applications. skos:exactMatch is a transitive property, and is a sub-property of skos:closeMatch.
Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core

a pour caractéristiques: symétrique, transitive

a pour super-propriétés
has close match op
a pour sous-propriétés
ORCID op, a un EDMO op, a un ROR op

is in mapping relation withop Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les propriétés d'objet

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#mappingRelation

These concept mapping relations mirror semantic relations, and the data model defined below is similar (with the exception of skos:exactMatch) to the data model defined for semantic relations. A distinct vocabulary is provided for concept mapping relations, to provide a convenient way to differentiate links within a concept scheme from links between concept schemes. However, this pattern of usage is not a formal requirement of the SKOS data model, and relies on informal definitions of best practice.
Relates two concepts coming, by convention, from different schemes, and that have comparable meanings
Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core
a pour super-propriétés
is in semantic relation with op
a pour sous-propriétés
has broader match op, has close match op, has narrower match op, has related match op

is in schemeop Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers les propriétés d'objet

IRI: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#inScheme

Relates a resource (for example a concept) to a concept scheme in which it is included.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core

is top concept in scheme op
a pour cible
Concept Scheme c

Est défini par
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core
a pour sous-propriétés
has broader transitive op, has narrower transitive op, has related op, is in mapping relation with op
a pour domaine
Concept c
a pour cible
Concept c

Atlantic Whale Dealni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#Atlantic_Whale_Deal

appartient à
Projet financé c
a pour assertions
homepage op atlantic whale deal ni
Project is led by an organisation op ARDITI ni
a pour pays op France ni
a pour pays op Ireland ni
a pour pays op Portugal ni
a pour pays op Spain ni
a un logo op Atlantic Whale Deal.png ni
a une emprise spatiale op Atlantic WhaleDeal Bouding box ni
End date dp "2026-11-30"^^date
Start date dp "2023-12-01"^^date
description ni "The ATLANTIC WHALE DEAL aims to make sure that ships don't accidentally hit whales in the Atlantic Area. Partners from different countries are working together to prevent these collisions that can damage biodiversity, trying out new and smart technologies to reduce ship strikes. They want to help the environment by capturing carbon (which is good for the planet) and making sure the ecosystem works well. This involves localization techniques to find whales, making maps to show where there's a risk of collisions, and creating tools to reduce noise in the ocean. A team of experts in physics, engineering, biology, statistics, design, and social economics from four countries is teaming up with different organizations like the government, non-profit groups, schools, and businesses. The goal is to find solutions that work well and help society while also taking care of the planet. ATLANTIC WHALE DEAL believes that by protecting whales, they're also making a positive impact on the world by keeping the environment healthy and preserving biodiversity."
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@el
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@en
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@es
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@fr
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@it
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@no
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@pt
title dp "Atlantic Whale Deal"@ro
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@el
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@en
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@es
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@fr
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@it
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@no
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@pt
preferred label ap "Atlantic Whale Deal"@ro

Black Seani Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#Black_Sea

The Black Sea is one of the largest regional seas of the Eurasian continent and unique in many of its geographical, geological, biological, hydrographical and socio-political characteristics. With anoxic conditions in the deep, problems with invasive species and high sediment loads delivered to the system, this area has unique problems requiring long-term stations. The Black Sea is located in a geological complex area, where three major tectonic plates (Eurasian, Anatolian, Arabian) interact. Geo hazards, such as earthquarkes, submarine landslide, displacement along active faults, are present and are possible triggers of tsunami, together with extreme meteorological events.
appartient à
Nœud régional c
est identique à
location_Black Sea ni
a pour assertions
Regional Facility is linked to a project op EUXINUS ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op EuxRo01 ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op EuxRo02 ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op EuxRo03 ni
Regional Facility has a bouding box op Black Sea Regional Facility bounding box ni
a une appartenance à un ERIC op Black Sea E M S O membership ni
Regional Faciliy has a Regional Team Leader op Vlad Rădulescu ni
Regional Facility is managed by an Organisation op GeoEcoMar ni
Distance from land dp "97"^^integer
Date du premier déploiement dp "2013-06-01"^^date
has max depth dp "95"^^integer
has min depth dp "15"^^integer
same As op "dbr:Black_Sea"
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@el
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@en
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@es
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@fr
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@it
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@no
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@pt
preferred label ap "Black Sea"@ro

Canariasni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#Canarias

Canarias Regional Facility is linked to the Atlantic Whale Deal project (issue concerning collision with marine mammals)
appartient à
Nœud régional c
est identique à
location_Canary islands ni
a pour assertions
Regional Facility is linked to a project op Atlantic Whale Deal ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op ESTOC ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op East of Gran Canaria ni
a une appartenance à un ERIC op Canarias E M S O membership ni
Regional Faciliy has a Regional Team Leader op Eric DELORY ni
Regional Facility is managed by an Organisation op CSIC ni
Regional Facility is managed by an Organisation op PLOCAN ni
Regional Facility is managed by an Organisation op ULPGC ni
homepage op https://plocan.eu/en/installations/ocean-observatory
Distance from land dp "60"^^integer
Date du premier déploiement dp "1994-01-01"
has max depth dp "3630"^^integer
description ni "EMSO Canarias, a.k.a. ESTOC (29º10’ N, 15º30’ W) is a monitoring time-series program. The station is located within the weak southward return flows on the Eastern side of the North Atlantic subtropical gyre. ESTOC is an open ocean site, located well outside of the highly variable Eastern boundary with its strong coastal upwelling regime (although interaction with this regime exists). The site is sufficiently deep to encompass the Eastern subtropical North Atlantic’s major water masses including the North Atlantic Deep Water (except the AABW). ESTOC is windward of the Canary Islands to avoid wake effects of both the major currents and winds (Canary Current and Northeast Trade Winds), and is far enough from coasts and islands to serve as reference for satellite images and altimetry."@en
preferred label ap "Canarias"@el
preferred label ap "Canarias"@en
preferred label ap "Canarias"@es
preferred label ap "Canarias"@fr
preferred label ap "Canarias"@it
preferred label ap "Canarias"@no
preferred label ap "Canarias"@pt
preferred label ap "Canarias"@ro

CLASSni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#CLASS

CLASS will deliver the knowledge and understanding of the Atlantic Ocean system that society needs to make evidence-based decisions regarding ocean management. We aim to address key knowledge gaps in our understanding of ocean variability, climate regulation and ocean services, and to assess how the ocean will evolve as a result of climate change and intensified human exploitation. CLASS will be delivered through an Atlantic focused science programme, building on sustained ocean observation, world class model development and state of the art technology, delivered through Marine National Capability underpinning activities.
appartient à
Projet financé c
a pour assertions
homepage op https://projects.noc.ac.uk/class-project/ ni
a pour pays op United Kingdom ni
a un logo op class.jpg ni
End date dp "2024-04"
Start date dp "2018-04"
description ni "CLASS will deliver the knowledge and understanding of the Atlantic Ocean system that society needs to make evidence-based decisions regarding ocean management. We aim to address key knowledge gaps in our understanding of ocean variability, climate regulation and ocean services, and to assess how the ocean will evolve as a result of climate change and intensified human exploitation. CLASS will be delivered through an Atlantic focused science programme, building on sustained ocean observation, world class model development and state of the art technology, delivered through Marine National Capability underpinning activities."
title dp "CLASS (Climate Linked Atlantic Sector Science)"@el
title dp "CLASS (Climate Linked Atlantic Sector Science)"@en
title dp "CLASS (Climate Linked Atlantic Sector Science)"@es

We aim to address key knowledge gaps in our understanding of ocean variability, climate regulation and ocean services, and to assess how the ocean will evolve as a result of climate change and intensified human exploitation. CLASS will be delivered through an Atlantic focused science programme, building on sustained ocean observation, world class model development and state of the art technology, delivered through Marine National Capability underpinning activities."@en

E1M3Ani Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#E1M3A

appartient à
Site d'observation c
a pour assertions
Site contains a Platform op E1M3A mooring ni
Site has a Site Responsible op Leonidas PERIVOLIOTIS ni
est un site de test dp "false"^^boolean
description ni "The E1M3A marine observatory is a pioneer station, the first open-ocean buoy in the Mediterranean deployed in January 2000 and currently part of the POSEIDON network since 2007. The Poseidon system operates a network of fixed measuring floats, which are moored at various locations in the Aegean and Ionian seas delivering near-real-time observations on important marine and atmospheric parameters. The oceanographic buoys constitute the backbone of the POSEIDON system and they are linked to both global and regional networks such as the OceanSITES and M3A’s respectively. In particular the POSEIDON E1 M3A buoy (WMO 61277) is the founder component of the Cretan Sea observatory complemented with several other platforms such as glider, FerryBox and a coastal buoy and is currently the most heavily equipped physical–biogeochemical observing site of the POSEIDON system."@en
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@el
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@en
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@es
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@fr
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@it
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@no
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@pt
preferred label ap "E1M3A"@ro

EUXINUSni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#EUXINUS

IOSIN EMSO-EUXINUS is a regional security system for the Western Black Sea with real-time alerting to marine geo-hazards (eg submarine landslides, active fault movements, submarine earthquakes, tsunami waves, etc.), which monitors characteristics and dynamics coastal water bodies, including a number of meteorological parameters. Romania is represented in the European multidisciplinary consortium for the observation and research of the ocean floor and water column - EMSO ERIC (full name-European Multidisciplinary Seafloor and Water Column Observatory - European Research Infrastructure Consortium), through the National Institute of Marine Geology and Geoecology - GeoEcoMar. It provides EMSO ERIC real-time data on the physico-chemical and biological parameters of the water column, at different depths, data on early warning in case of marine hazards, as well as the experience of personnel involved in the proper functioning of the entire system. . The source of the provided data is the EUXINUS network of marine and coastal observatories (3 offshore and one coastal observatories), part of the Black Sea Security System, and their storage is done in the database within the National Center for Monitoring - Alarming to Marine Natural Hazards. , which operates within the Constanta branch of INCD GeoEcoMar.
appartient à
Projet financé c
a pour assertions
Project is led by an organisation op GeoEcoMar ni
a pour pays op Romania ni
has location op Black Sea ni
homepage op https://www.euxinus.eu/index_en.html
alternative label ap "IOSIN EMSO-EUXINUS network"@en
definition ap "IOSIN EMSO-EUXINUS is a regional security system for the Western Black Sea with real-time alerting to marine geo-hazards (eg submarine landslides, active fault movements, submarine earthquakes, tsunami waves, etc.), which monitors characteristics and dynamics coastal water bodies, including a number of meteorological parameters. Romania is represented in the European multidisciplinary consortium for the observation and research of the ocean floor and water column - EMSO ERIC (full name-European Multidisciplinary Seafloor and Water Column Observatory - European Research Infrastructure Consortium), through the National Institute of Marine Geology and Geoecology - GeoEcoMar. It provides EMSO ERIC real-time data on the physico-chemical and biological parameters of the water column, at different depths, data on early warning in case of marine hazards, as well as the experience of personnel involved in the proper functioning of the entire system. . The source of the provided data is the EUXINUS network of marine and coastal observatories (3 offshore and one coastal observatories), part of the Black Sea Security System, and their storage is done in the database within the National Center for Monitoring - Alarming to Marine Natural Hazards. , which operates within the Constanta branch of INCD GeoEcoMar."@en
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@el
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@en
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@es
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@fr
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@it
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@no
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@pt
preferred label ap "EUXINUS"@ro

Iberian Marginni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#Iberian_Margin

appartient à
Nœud régional c
est identique à
location_Gulf of Cadiz ni
a pour assertions
Regional Facility is part of a Research Infrastructure op EMSO Portugal ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op IbMa-CSV Sao Vicente Canyon ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op IbMa-CSV Seabed Station ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op IbMa-CSV TUPEM Fixed SubSurface Vertical Profiler ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op IbMa-CS TUPEM SubSurface Mooring ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op IbMa-N ni
a une appartenance à un ERIC op Iberian Margin E M S O membership ni
Regional Faciliy has a Regional Team Leader op Carlos SOUSA ni
Regional Facility is managed by an Organisation op IPMA ni
homepage op https://emso-pt.pt/
homepage op https://emso.eu/observatories-node/iberian-margin/
Distance from land dp "11"^^integer
Date du premier déploiement dp "2021-05-01"
has max depth dp "1200"^^integer
description ni "The area of Gulf of Cadiz is very important for geo-hazards. It was the site where one of the worst earthquakes that hit Europe occurred in 1755, coupled with a destructive tsunami. Here African and European tectonic plates converge. It is a seismic volcanic region. The Iberian Margin is also a pivotal ocean region where Atlantic and Mediterranean exchange waters, with the later spreading at intermediate levels playing a key role in the salt and heat budget and AMOC (Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation). The Iberian Margin is the northernmost region of the productive CCUS (Canary Current Upwelling System), one of the four EBUS (Eastern Boundary Upwelling Systems) of the global ocean, where a highly variable upwelling regime dominates along with a strong mesoscale activity. The geologic and oceanographic features of this region favour the presence of highly diverse benthic communities and have also a central role in the distribution of several marine mammals and fish species."@en
note ap "IbMa"@en
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@el
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@en
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@es
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@fr
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@it
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@no
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@pt
preferred label ap "Iberian Margin"@ro

Ligurian Seani Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#Ligurian_Sea

The Ligurian Sea is an active seismic region surrounded by a narrow and steep continental slope and alpine reliefs. Ligurian seismic events are not rare for seismic hazard evaluation in the Nice area and a similar event could be occurred along the margin at the level of the French-Italian border. Such event will produce destruction in a zone which is presently much more densely populated and could also be associated with a tsunami along the coast. The region is also characterized by the presence of rivers becoming streams after heavy rainfall. This promotes slope instability and the formation of submarine canyons. The Ligurian Sea is also strongly influenced by atmospheric inputs triggering changes in biomass production.
appartient à
Nœud régional c
est identique à
location_French Riviera ni, location_Gulf of Lion ni
a pour assertions
Regional Facility is part of a Research Infrastructure op EMSO-France ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op BILLION ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op DYFAMED ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op LION ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op LO ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op Nice ni
Regional Facility has a bouding box op Ligurian Sea Regional Facility bounding box ni
a une appartenance à un ERIC op Ligurian Sea E M S O membership ni
Regional Faciliy has a Regional Team Leader op Laurent COPPOLA ni

MARMORni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#MARMOR

MARMOR (Marine Advanced geophysical Research equipment and Mayotte multidisciplinary Observatory for research and Response) est un projet d'une durée de 8 ans financé par l'ANR Le projet MARMOR a un double objectif : Structurer la communauté scientifique française, en fournissant à l'Infrastructure de Recherche Epos-France (ex Résif) les équipements nécessaires pour conduire des recherches d’excellence utilisant les outils de la sismologie et de la géodésie sous-marines. Mettre en place une infrastructure d’observation sous-marine à Mayotte pour assurer les impératifs de surveillance sismologique continue, temps-réel et 24/7 et conduire des recherches multidisciplinaires sur les phénomènes associés à la crise sismo-volcanique en cours.
appartient à
Projet financé c
a pour assertions
a pour pays op France ni
has location op Mayotte ni
a un logo op marmor.png ni
homepage op https://www.marmor-project.org/fr
alternative label ap "Marine Advanced geophysical Research equipment and Mayotte multidisciplinary Observatory for research and Response"@en
definition ap "MARMOR (Marine Advanced geophysical Research equipment and Mayotte multidisciplinary Observatory for research and Response) est un projet d'une durée de 8 ans financé par l'ANR Le projet MARMOR a un double objectif : Structurer la communauté scientifique française, en fournissant à l'Infrastructure de Recherche Epos-France (ex Résif) les équipements nécessaires pour conduire des recherches d’excellence utilisant les outils de la sismologie et de la géodésie sous-marines. Mettre en place une infrastructure d’observation sous-marine à Mayotte pour assurer les impératifs de surveillance sismologique continue, temps-réel et 24/7 et conduire des recherches multidisciplinaires sur les phénomènes associés à la crise sismo-volcanique en cours."@fr
note ap "MARMOR"@en
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@el
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@en
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@es
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@fr
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@it
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@no
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@pt
preferred label ap "MARMOR"@ro

Nordic Seasni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#Nordic_Seas

The Nordic Seas regional facility comprises 5 ocean glider sections, 4 mooring sites, and one multi array observatory. The facility is a mixture of existing monitoring components, which are expanded, and new establishments and the main aim is to contribute to increased understanding of the role of the Nordic Seas in the global climate system. Particularly, the multidisciplinary research conducted at Nordic Seas regional facility focuses on: Water transport, circulation and water mass properties of the Nordic Seas Sub-surface biogeochemistry • Methane release from seafloor Front variability between Polar and Atlantic Water Geophysical, oceanographic and ecological characterization of a hydrothermal vent The activity at the Nordic Seas regional facility is shown in the map, where the ocean glider transects are indicated with red lines and numbers, the mooring sites are indicated with white circles and named Svinøy, Station M, South Cape, and Fram Strait, and the multi array observatory is indicated with a white circle and named EMSO-Mohn. EMSO scientific disciplines: physical oceanography, biogeochemistry, marine ecology (planned)
appartient à
Nœud régional c
est identique à
location_Norwegian Sea ni
a pour assertions
Regional Facility is part of a Research Infrastructure op NorEMSO ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op Central Fram Strait ni
Regional Facility contains a Site op Mohn ni

Smartbay Observatoryni Retour à Table des matières ou Retour vers l'individu nommé

IRI: https://w3id.org/earthsemantics/OSO#Smartbay_observatory

The SmartBay cabled observatory is connected to a shorestation via a 5km long subsea fibre optic power cable. This cable provides power to the observatory and facilitates high speed communication with the various instruments and sensors deployed. The observatory operates a core suite of sensors which measure several EOVs and other relevant environmental indicators. These instruments include CTD, Ph, dissolved oxygen, pco2, hydrophones, HD camera, flow cytometer and ADCP. These instruments are cabled to a central electronics node. This node also includes several additional ports and cables with multiple power and communications configurations which are available to host other novel sensors and instruments. The testing and demonstration of new and novel equipment is one of the main drivers behind the SmartBay facility, allowing researchers and industry the ability to trial their technology in the marine environment whilst also being in real time communication with their equipment at all times. Environmental data recorded by the various infrastructures at the SmartBay facility is streamed in real time to Marine Institute servers and subsequently to various online platforms and databases. The online data currently includes live video and audio, currents, wave data and feeds from the several instruments currently deployed.
appartient à
Plateforme d'observation c
a pour assertions
a pour catégorie de plateforme op http://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/L06/current/11/ ni
Platform has a Principal Investigator op Alan BERRY ni
Date du premier déploiement dp "2015-10-08"^^date
alternative label ap "Galway Bay Cabled Observatory"@en
definition ap "The SmartBay cabled observatory is connected to a shorestation via a 5km long subsea fibre optic power cable. This cable provides power to the observatory and facilitates high speed communication with the various instruments and sensors deployed. The observatory operates a core suite of sensors which measure several EOVs and other relevant environmental indicators. These instruments include CTD, Ph, dissolved oxygen, pco2, hydrophones, HD camera, flow cytometer and ADCP. These instruments are cabled to a central electronics node. This node also includes several additional ports and cables with multiple power and communications configurations which are available to host other novel sensors and instruments. The testing and demonstration of new and novel equipment is one of the main drivers behind the SmartBay facility, allowing researchers and industry the ability to trial their technology in the marine environment whilst also being in real time communication with their equipment at all times. Environmental data recorded by the various infrastructures at the SmartBay facility is streamed in real time to Marine Institute servers and subsequently to various online platforms and databases. The online data currently includes live video and audio, currents, wave data and feeds from the several instruments currently deployed."@en

preferred label ap "Smartbay Observatory"@en

preferred label ap "Smartbay Observatory"@no
preferred label ap "Smartbay Observatory"@pt
preferred label ap "Smartbay Observatory"@ro
